item.title: Chinese Manufacturing Growth Overtaken by Finance Amid IPO Boom
item.description: Chinese manufacturing grew slower than finance for the first time in years, turbocharged by capital raised through share sales during a quarter that saw a surprise rebound in the economy.

item.title: Hong Kong Bourse Plans to Start Zero-Day Options in Early 2027
item.description: Hong Kong’s stock exchange is working on introducing options expiring within a day next year, joining a global trend toward shorter-dated bets that’s sent US derivatives volume ballooning.

item.title: CATL Share Sale’s Tightening Discount Shows China Tech Momentum
item.description: Contemporary Amperex Technology Co.’s latest share sale — priced at a 5.1% discount — was more than twice subscribed. That underscores strong investor appetite for the stock even after the battery maker’s 20% rally this year.

item.title: It Doesn’t Matter What Kevin Warsh Has to Say
item.description: The Powell case and future economic challenges are out of the Fed nominee’s hands.

item.title: Mercuria Seeks New Liquidity in Asia With $200 Million Financing
item.description: Mercuria Energy Group Ltd. plans to raise at least $200 million of financing in Asia, according to people familiar with the matter, as commodity traders seek alternative sources of funding at a time when the Iran war has raised the cost of buying cargoes.

item.title: Kevin Warsh Heads for the Hill: A Confirmation Hearing Guide
item.description: Kevin Warsh, President Donald Trump’s nominee to chair the Federal Reserve, will appear before the Senate Banking Committee for a confirmation hearing at 10 a.m. Tuesday. It could be the most contentious such hearing in many decades.

item.title: Congo to Halt Cash Dollar Use Next Year to Prop Local Currency
item.description: Democratic Republic of Congo’s central bank will restrict cash transactions in US dollars starting next April to curb money laundering and improve confidence in the national currency.

item.title: China’s Wanhua Bets on Overseas Growth as Trade Risks Mount
item.description: China’s Wanhua Chemical Group Co. pledged to accelerate its overseas expansion to help counter rising trade risks after the petrochemical giant reported an increase in first-quarter earnings.

item.title: South Korean Battery Stocks Rally on Mercedes-Benz Partnership
item.description: Shares of South Korea’s top battery makers jumped Tuesday after clinching deals with Mercedes-Benz Group AG, providing a welcome boost to a sector that has been hit by the slowing global transition to electric vehicles.

item.title: Hedge Funds Beef Up Bearish Dollar Bets as Haven Demand Sinks
item.description: Hedge funds and other asset managers are ramping up bets on dollar weakness using options, in a sign the market is turning even further against the US currency as haven demand wanes.

item.title: Chinese Copper Output Hits Record on Tailwind From Sulfuric Acid
item.description: Copper smelters in China churned out a record volume of refined metal last month, as surging prices of byproduct sulfuric acid encouraged higher output and aided the industry’s profitability.

item.title: Indian Stocks Face Fresh Hurdle as Fuel Price-Hike Risk Looms
item.description: If pump prices do rise, the effects will be felt across the broader economy.

item.title: JPMorgan Aims for China Approval of Active ETF Launch This Year
item.description: JPMorgan Chase & Co. is working toward getting approval from Chinese securities regulators to launch actively managed exchange-traded funds in the country for the first time.

item.title: Australian Gasoline Prices Fall for Third Straight Week on Government Interventions
item.description: Australian gasoline prices fell for a third straight week, as government measures eased some of the pain at the pump triggered by the war in Iran.

item.title: Sanrio Launches Gaming Brand, Tapping Fast-Growing Global Market
item.description: Hello Kitty creator Sanrio Co. is launching a new gaming brand as it looks to monetize its portfolio of popular characters and tap into the fast-growing global games market.

item.title: Jeff Bezos Nears $10 Billion Funding Round for AI Lab, FT Says
item.description: Jeff Bezos is close to finalizing a $10 billion funding round for his AI startup that’s developing models with the capability of understanding the physical world, the Financial Times reports.

item.title: Bank of Korea’s New Chief Signals Flexible Policy Amid Oil Risks
item.description: South Korea’s new central bank chief signaled a cautious and flexible approach to monetary policy as higher oil prices add to inflation pressures while clouding the outlook for economic growth.

item.title: MTR Markets First Public HK Dollar Bond, Adding to Rush of Deals
item.description: MTR Corp Ltd. began marketing its first-ever Hong Kong dollar public bond offering Tuesday, adding to an uptick of borrowing in the local currency debt market.

item.title: China’s Aluminum Output Fueled by Middle East’s Stranded Cargoes
item.description: Chinese imports of the main raw material for aluminum jumped to a two-year high in March, as cargoes bound for smelters in the Middle East were rerouted to the world’s biggest producer.

item.title: Japan Scraps Most Weapons Export Limits in Boost to Arms Sector
item.description: Japan removed most of its restrictions on weapons exports as it seeks to create a larger market for companies in the arms business while building a more robust base of suppliers for its own military.

item.title: Apple to Focus Hardware Team on Five Areas Under Johny Srouji
item.description: Apple Inc.’s newly combined hardware engineering and hardware technologies division will be organized across five key areas, its new leader told staffers on Monday.

item.title: South Korean Stock Index Rises to Record as AI Optimism Extends
item.description: South Korean stocks climbed to a record, powered by chipmakers as the artificial intelligence trade regained momentum and optimism reemerged about a US-Iran peace deal.

item.title: South Korea’s Early Trade Data Show Export Momentum Still Strong
item.description: South Korea’s export growth remained robust in early April, signaling resilient external demand even as surging oil prices and a weaker currency raise risks for inflation and growth.

item.title: EQT Raises Record $15.6 Billion for Asia Private Equity Fund
item.description: EQT AB raised $15.6 billion for its latest private equity Asia fund, the largest pool of capital ever assembled for the region, as global investors look beyond the US amid elevated uncertainty.

item.title: MSCI Delays High-Stakes Indonesia Review After Downgrade Fears Spooked Investors
item.description: MSCI Inc. will maintain a string of measures on Indonesian equities and provide an update on the nation’s market status in June, saying that it’s evaluating the reforms rolled out by regulators.

item.title: Nvidia Supplier Victory Giant Set for Debut After Biggest Hong Kong Listing This Year
item.description: Victory Giant Technology Huizhou Co., a Chinese supplier of Nvidia Corp., is set to begin trading in Hong Kong on Tuesday after raising $2.6 billion in the city’s largest listing in seven months.

item.title: China Flashes New Tech Swagger to World Markets Convulsed by War
item.description: At the world’s largest trade show, it’s not just the clientele that had a different look this year.

item.title: New Zealand Inflation Above 1-3% Target Ahead of Fuel Shock
item.description: New Zealand inflation unexpectedly stayed above the Reserve Bank’s 1 — 3% target in the first quarter, even before the expected Iran-war driven surge in consumer prices from higher fuel and other costs.

item.title: Steel Dynamics Revenue Grows Most Since 2022, Beating Estimates
item.description: Steel Dynamics Inc. beat analysts’ estimates for first-quarter revenue, reporting its fastest growth in nearly four years as rising steel prices and a record 3.6 million tons in steel shipments drove a rebound in profit.

item.title: Rio Tinto Copper Production Rises as Oyu Tolgoi Ramps Up
item.description: Rio Tinto Group’s copper production rose 9% in the first quarter, thanks to a ramp-up at the Oyu Tolgoi copper mine in Mongolia, while iron ore output in Australia also climbed in spite of seasonal weather disruptions.
